title: --onbuild "COPY" fails with --chown and --chmod
body:
### Issue Description

`buildah config --onbuild="COPY --chown"` and `buildah config --onbuild="COPY --chmod"` fail.

Possibly not being parsed correctly, thus tried escaping and rearranging the string, but to no avail.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

test1
```
ctr=$(buildah from --format=docker -- "ubuntu:plucky")

buildah config --onbuild="COPY setup.sh /setup.sh" $ctr

buildah commit --format docker $ctr test

buildah from test
```
Result:
```
STEP 1: COPY setup.sh /setup.sh
test-working-container
```
test2
```
ctr=$(buildah from --format=docker -- "ubuntu:plucky")

buildah config --onbuild="COPY --chown=1000:1000 setup.sh /setup.sh" $ctr

buildah commit --format docker $ctr test

buildah from test
```
Result:
```
STEP 1: COPY --chown=1000:1000 setup.sh /setup.sh
Error: checking on sources under "/": copier: stat: "/home/user/Documents/--chown=1000:1000": no such file or directory
```

### Describe the results you received

Failure

### Describe the results you expected

Success

### buildah version output

```yaml
Version:         1.39.3
Go Version:      go1.24.4
Image Spec:      1.1.0
Runtime Spec:    1.2.0
CNI Spec:        1.0.0
libcni Version:  
image Version:   5.34.2
Git Commit:      
Built:           Wed Dec 31 19:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
BuildPlatform:   linux/amd64
```

### buildah info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run `buildah info` for any reason, please provide the operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.
```

### Provide your storage.conf

```toml
storage.conf
```

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Debian 12 Bookworm

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Hello @tabletseeker , i was interested in this issue, so i tried to dig deeper into the source code to understand this behaviour . And it was pretty clear why this was happening.
In the from.go file , in the cmd/buildah directory , we do have a function called onBuild , that will be invoked if we use the from command on an image that have the onbuild embedded in it's config , this is a code snippet from that function , located in the [line 122](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/cmd/buildah/from.go#L122):
```
switch command {
		case "ADD":
		case "COPY":
			dest := ""
			size := len(args)
			if size > 1 {
				dest = args[size-1]
				args = args[:size-1]
			}
			if err := builder.Add(dest, command == "ADD", buildah.AddAndCopyOptions{}, args...); err != nil {
				return err
			}
```
One interesting argument is the AddAndCopyOptions struct , which has this definition:
```
type AddAndCopyOptions struct {
	Chmod string
	Chown string
	Checksum string
	PreserveOwnership bool
	......
        ......
}

```
It's clear that this struct is the one that contains the chmod , chown and the rest of the arguments , but an empty struct is being passed , leading to those arguemnts being ignored (or to be more precise , the first argument in the COPY will be considered as the destination and the rest as the sources) . That's why ,in the Add method, the --chown=1000:1000 is being treated as a source file or directory location. (You can consult the Add method to further understand what's happening).

Being able to correctly parse those values when using the --onbuild flag seems pretty valuable , and i am not sure if i am missing somthing @nalind  @Luap99 @flouthoc .

I can see it's not as straightforward as in the copy or add commands , since in those we can just retrieve the values from the flags , but i believe i can do it in a clean way , I can work on a patch for that if you see that this is valuable.

@GGh41th  Hey man, thanks for the reply. Right, that makes sense. I've since solved this with my own dirty fix, but I'm sure you'll have a more elegant approach. Just as you said, the arg immediately following `COPY`.

Please let me know if I can help in any way, I'd love to contribute to this project.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
